method_atom: a variational field theory in which the governing action is a nonlinear function of a curvature-like invariant, formulated with two independent geometric structures — a metric-like symmetric object and an independent connection carrying an antisymmetric (twist) part — varied separately. The resulting extra degrees of freedom, beyond those of the minimal linear theory, can be algebraically re-encoded as an equivalent effective auxiliary scalar field of purely geometric origin, both in vacuum and in the presence of a structureless continuous medium source.
object_atom: the geometry of spacetime and its gravitational dynamics, treated as f(R) gravity extended with torsion in the metric-affine (Palatini-like) formalism, applied cosmologically to model the dark energy and dark matter content of the Universe with empty space and perfect-fluid matter.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the nonlinear-action, independently-varied-connection-with-twist formalism whose surplus degrees of freedom recast as an effective scalar field; the domain-specific object is spacetime gravity and cosmology, which are the general-relativity subject the machinery acts on.
